context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same sorting logic used by the module that handles 'extends' lines
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This refers to a lexicographical sort order derived from the output generation logic for the 'extends' section, implying the same custom comparator or dictionary-based ordering used elsewhere in the output formatter.

context_pointers.vague_reference: normalize names according to the convention from the core transformation layer
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This requires using the specific dotted notation convention (e.g., 'Package.Sub.Primitive') defined in the core naming rule implementation, rather than the raw tokenized names from the AST.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_contribute_to.json.description: A public interface annotated as a scope contribution generates a component-interface stub in the lookup package whose origin is the source type and which extends that source interface. Non-public or non-interface declarations are rejected with a domain reason.

sources:
package com.example

import software.amazon.lastmile.kotlin.inject.anvil.ContributesTo

@ContributesTo(Unit::class)
private interface ComponentInterface

cases.expected_output:
status=rejected
error=invalid_usage
reason=Contributed component interfaces must be public.

feature2_binding_validation.json.description: Binding contribution validates the bound type and scope: the bound type must be unambiguous (exactly one super type), the same type may not be bound twice, and all scope-defining annotations must agree.

cases.expected_output:
status=rejected
error=invalid_usage
reason=The bound type could not be determined for Impl. There are no super types.

feature2_contributes_binding.json.description: An injectable implementation annotated as a binding generates a provider that exposes it under its super type. The provider direction (from implementation to bound type) and the binding kind (single vs multibinding set element) are observable.

sources:
package com.example

import software.amazon.lastmile.kotlin.inject.anvil.ContributesBinding
import me.tatarka.inject.annotations.Inject

interface Base

@Inject
@ContributesBinding(Unit::class)
class Impl : Base

cases.expected_output:
status=compiled
contribution origin=com.example.Impl
provides from=com.example.Impl to=com.example.Base kind=single

sources:
package com.example

import software.amazon.lastmile.kotlin.inject.anvil.ContributesBinding
import me.tatarka.inject.annotations.Inject

interface Base

@Inject
@ContributesBinding(Unit::class, multibinding = true)
class Impl : Base

cases.expected_output:
status=compiled
contribution origin=com.example.Impl
provides from=com.example.Impl to=com.example.Base kind=multi

feature3_merge_component.json.description: A component annotated to merge a scope collects every contribution for that scope; the generated merged super-interface inherits the contributed component interfaces (a nested one and a top-level one), proving the contributions were actually merged into the component.

feature3_merge_validation.json.description: Merging validates the wiring: a manually @Component-wired class that merges a scope must add the generated merged super-interface, and excluding a contribution the component still depends on surfaces an unresolved-dependency reason.

sources:
package com.example

import me.tatarka.inject.annotations.Component
import software.amazon.lastmile.kotlin.inject.anvil.AppScope
import software.amazon.lastmile.kotlin.inject.anvil.MergeComponent

@Component
@MergeComponent(AppScope::class)
abstract class ComponentInterface

cases.expected_output:
status=rejected
error=invalid_usage
reason=ComponentInterface is annotated with @MergeComponent and @Component. It's required to add ComponentInterfaceMerged as super type to ComponentInterface. If you don't want to add the super manually, then you must remove the @Component annotation.

feature4_create_component_validation.jso.description: The common-multiplatform factory function that materializes a merged component has strict shape rules: it must be public and use the expect modifier, its return type must itself be a merge target (not hand-wired with @Component), and a receiver, if present, must be the KClass of the component with matching argument and arity.

sources:
package com.example

import software.amazon.lastmile.kotlin.inject.anvil.AppScope
import software.amazon.lastmile.kotlin.inject.anvil.MergeComponent

@MergeComponent(AppScope::class)
interface ComponentInterface

@MergeComponent.CreateComponent
internal expect fun createComponent(): ComponentInterface

cases.expected_output:
status=rejected
error=invalid_usage
reason=Factory functions for components annotated with `@CreateComponent` must be public.

feature5_subcomponent_factory.json.description: A contributed subcomponent declares a nested factory interface; a factory component stub is generated into the lookup package, named after the fully-qualified factory type, whose origin is the factory and which provides the factory into its parent scope.

cases.expected_output:
status=compiled
contribution origin=com.example.SubcomponentInterface.Factory
extends com.example.SubcomponentInterface.Factory
provides from=none to=com.example.SubcomponentInterface.Factory kind=single

feature5_subcomponent_validation.json.description: Subcomponent contribution enforces structural rules: the contributed type must be an interface, it must declare exactly one factory inner interface, the factory must contain exactly one function returning the subcomponent, and a kotlin-inject scope must be present.

cases.expected_output:
status=rejected
error=invalid_usage
reason=Only interfaces can be contributed. If you have parameters on your abstract class, then move them to the factory. See @ContributesSubcomponent for more details.

feature6_contributing_annotation.json.description: Marking a user-defined annotation as a contributing annotation is accepted and compiles successfully, generating a cross-module discovery marker for the custom annotation in the lookup package.

feature6_contributing_annotation_validat.description: A user-defined annotation marked as contributing must be public; a non-public annotation is rejected with a domain reason.

cases.expected_output:
status=rejected
error=invalid_usage
reason=Contributing annotations must be public.

_constraints.output_rule: First line must be 'status=rejected'. Second line must be 'error=invalid_usage'

_constraints.input_condition: User requests report for a specific type via 'inspect' field
_constraints.output_rule: For each successful inspection, emit 'contribution origin=<fqn>' followed by sorted 'extends <fqn>' lines

_constraints.input_condition: Type is a binding contribution (ContributesBinding) with a concrete bound type
Type is a subcomponent factory contribution
_constraints.output_rule: Emit 'provides from=none to=<factory_fqn> kind=single'. Does not emit 'extends' lines for the factory itself unless specified.

_constraints.input_condition: User requests report for a component via 'merged' field
_constraints.output_rule: Emit 'merges <fqn>' for each contributing type folded in.

_constraints.input_condition: User requests report for an annotation via 'annotation' field

_constraints.output_rule: Third line (or subsequent unique lines) must start with 'reason=', followed by a domain sentence. No file paths, stack traces, or host types.

_constraints.input_condition: A interface is annotated with ContributesTo
_constraints.output_rule: Must be public. Private modifiers result in rejection
exact_values: Contributed component interfaces must be public.

_constraints.output_rule: The class must extend exactly one interface. No super types results in rejection.
exact_values: The bound type could not be determined for <class>. There are no super types.

_constraints.input_condition: ContributesBinding annotation is used with multibinding=true
A class is annotated with @MergeComponent and @Component
_constraints.output_rule: Must extend ComponentInterfaceMerged. Missing extension results in rejection.
exact_values: It's required to add ComponentInterfaceMerged as super type to <Class>. If you don't want to add the super manually, then you must remove the @Component annotation.

_constraints.output_rule: Must be public. Internal keywords result in rejection
exact_values: Factory functions for components annotated with `@CreateComponent` must be public.

_constraints.input_condition: A type is annotated as ContributesSubcomponent
_constraints.output_rule: Must be an interface. Abstract classes result in rejection.

_constraints.input_condition: An annotation is marked with ContributingAnnotation
_constraints.output_rule: Must be public. Internal modifiers result in rejection

_constraints.input_condition: Any group of lines (extends, merges, provides)
_constraints.output_rule: Lines within a group must be sorted lexicographically

_constraints.input_condition: Source file text parsed by processor
_constraints.output_rule: Nested type names in output must be normalized to dotted form (e.g., InnerClassName.Array)
Each case produces one stdout block; total execution is in a single process
